content.problemStatement: In this task, we will count the number of `vowels` and `consonants` in a given sentence. This helps in analyzing the composition of text.
constraints: Only standard English letters are considered; all other characters are ignored
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a sentence S (0 ≤ |S| ≤ 100). The line may be empty and can contain spaces, digits, punctuation, etc.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output two lines:
Vowels: X
Consonants: Y
where X is the number of alphabetic characters that are vowels (a, e, i, o, u, case‑insensitive) and Y is the number of alphabetic characters that are consonants.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The sentence contains 6 vowels (I, a, e, a, i, o) and 11 consonants (m, l, r, n, n, g, P, y, t, h, n).
‘+’ is not a letter. Vowels are I, a, e, a, i (5). Consonants are m, l, r, n, n, g, C (7).
First line: vowels e, o, o (3); consonants H,l,l,W,r,l,d (7). Second line: all ten letters are vowels.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line as a string. Initialise two counters vowelCnt and consCnt to zero. Iterate over every character c in the string. If c is an alphabetic character (use isalpha / Character.isLetter / str.isalpha / /[a-zA-Z]/) then check whether it belongs to the vowel set {a,e,i,o,u} ignoring case. If it is a vowel increment vowelCnt, otherwise increment consCnt. After processing the line output the two counters in the required format. The algorithm is linear in the length of the input string and uses only O(1) additional memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of characters in all test cases) – each character is examined once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    T = int(data[0].strip())
    vowels = set('aeiouAEIOU')
    out = []
    for i in range(1, T + 1):
        s = data[i] if i < len(data) else ''
        v = sum(1 for ch in s if ch.isalpha() and ch in vowels)
        c = sum(1 for ch in s if ch.isalpha() and ch not in vowels)
        out.append(f"Vowels: {v}")
        out.append(f"Consonants: {c}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
